projectDescription: BeeNut is an open-source computer-vision counting kiosk that turns any desktop machine, Raspberry Pi, or single-board computer into a dedicated object-counting appliance powered by custom YOLO ONNX models.
keyFeatures: Touch-friendly Flutter kiosk UI for live object counting
Bring-your-own YOLO ONNX model support with hot-reload
Native GStreamer camera preview with zero-copy transport (IOSurface, DMA-BUF, Shared Memory)
Stable gated counting with confidence filtering and temporal smoothing
Raspberry Pi GPIO control for tray sensors and light relays
Multi-platform packaging: macOS, Linux desktop, and Raspberry Pi appliance
Target catalog for part type management
Built-in diagnostics and system resource monitoring
publicApiSurface: install-linux.sh (one-command installer with BEENUT_VERSION and BEENUT_PROFILE)
Install profiles: appliance-pi, appliance-linux, desktop, dev-service
config.json (MachineConfig: camera, model, gpio, counting, ui, part_types)
IPC protocol (JSON-lines over Unix socket at /tmp/beenutd.sock)
KioskServiceClient interface: selectPartType, saveConfig, setPreviewPaused, runDiagnostic, shutdown
BEENUT_GPIO_ALLOW=1 environment flag for non-Pi GPIO testing
